Role_in_PV_Module: Front protective layer; provides rigidity, high optical transmission, physical protection from the elements.
Key_Properties: Low Fe composition; Tempered for durability; chemically stable; high transmissivity; high melting point.
Recycling_Considerations: High mass but low value; EVA separation is the main barrier to recovery.

Role_in_PV_Module: Primary conductor in PV interconnection. Used in busbars & internal wiring. Potential to replace silver in solar cell metallization.
Key_Properties: High strength & hardness; great electrical conductivity; great heat conductivity
Recycling_Considerations: Often tightly bonded to other materials, which can hinder recycling process. EVA layer is also a barrier to accessing the silicon wafer and other metals.

Role_in_PV_Module: Encapsulates and protects cells from atmospheric influences, vibrations, and humidity; bonds module layers together.
Key_Properties: Durable at high temperatures; High bonding strength; Cross‑linked polymer; Flexible; Difficult to delaminate during recycling.
Recycling_Considerations: Major barrier to recycling due to bonding and cross-linking; requires thermal or chemical removal.

Role_in_PV_Module: Used in mounting hardware, racking, and some structural components.
Key_Properties: High strength; Highly malleable & ductile; Magnetic; Widely recyclable with established infrastructure.
Recycling_Considerations: Highly recyclable; contributes to circularity in mounting systems.

Role_in_PV_Module: Commonly used for module frames due to high strength-to-weight ratio.
Key_Properties: Lightweight; corrosion‑resistant; high embodied energy; excellent recyclability.
Recycling_Considerations: One of the most economically valuable components; easily recovered and recycled.
